findings.title: JSON schema parser accepts unknown enum names when ordinals are absent
findings.suggested_fix: Treat JSON route artifacts as untrusted at the schema boundary: require route.version === SCHEMA_VERSION, require leg.venue and leg.amountMode to be own keys of VenueFamily and AmountMode, require numeric ordinal fields to exist and match, and add negative tests for unknown names with missing ordinals.

findings.title: Production materialization silently changes exact intermediate legs to All
findings.suggested_fix: Make dynamic-input conversion an explicit artifact/schema property instead of inferring it from tokenIn != route.tokenIn. Preserve Exact intermediate amounts by default, and add a materialization test for routes where an intermediate asset is partially spent and later reused.

residual_risks: I did not run forge, bun, or fork tests because this reviewer was constrained to read-only commands except writing the requested artifact.

The branch keeps schema version 1 while appending venue ordinals 6 and 7; downstream app safety still depends on the documented www-side schema parity gate before removing NativeWrap or Aerodrome V2 blockers.
Live Universal Router and Permit2 behavior was assessed statically; final confidence still depends on the pinned Base fork route suite and live deployment validation.
testing_gaps: No negative router-ts test covers routeFromJson with an unknown venue or amountMode and missing ordinal fields.
No production materialization test covers a split or merge route that must preserve an Exact intermediate amount for a later leg.
No consumer-facing test proves production callers reject or re-materialize fork-evidence artifacts before submitting calldata.
